questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aldehydes and ketones can undergo oxidation to form carboxylic acids.
questions.reason: Reason (R): During oxidation, the aldehyde or ketone group is converted into a carboxyl group, resulting in the formation of a carboxylic acid.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because aldehydes and ketones can undergo oxidation to form carboxylic acids, which involves the conversion of the carbonyl group into a carboxyl group.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Aldehydes have a hydrogen atom attached to the carbonyl carbon, making them more reactive than ketones.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because the presence of a hydrogen atom attached to the carbonyl carbon in aldehydes makes them more reactive than ketones.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Carboxylic acids can be reduced to form alcohols.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The carboxyl group in carboxylic acids can be reduced to form an alcohol group, resulting in the formation of an alcohol.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because the carboxyl group in carboxylic acids can be reduced to form an alcohol group, resulting in the formation of an alcohol.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Aldehydes and ketones are polar molecules.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The carbonyl group in aldehydes and ketones is polar, resulting in the formation of a polar molecule.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because the carbonyl group in aldehydes and ketones is polar, resulting in the formation of a polar molecule.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Carboxylic acids can undergo decarboxylation.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Decarboxylation involves the removal of the carboxyl group from a carboxylic acid, resulting in the formation of a hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because decarboxylation involves the removal of the carboxyl group from a carboxylic acid, resulting in the formation of a hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Carboxylic acids are weak acids.
